en.extract:
The tawny-backed fantail (Rhipidura superflua), also known as the cinnamon-backed fantail, is a species of bird in the family Rhipiduridae. It is endemic to Buru island in Indonesia. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical moist montane forests. The species occupies an area of larger than 20,000 km2 and has a stable population of above 10,000, and thus is not considered as threatened.

The '''tawny-backed fantail''' ('''''Rhipidura superflua'''''), also known as the '''cinnamon-backed fantail''', is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Rhipiduridae]]. It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Buru]] island in [[Indonesia]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]s. The species occupies an area of larger than 20,000 km<sup>2</sup> and has a stable population of above 10,000, and thus is not considered as threatened.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />
